concept.conceptName: Gamma glutamyl transferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma

en.name: Gamma glutamyl transferase (GGT) in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Gamma glutamyl transferase (GGT) is an enzyme found mainly in the liver, bile ducts, and kidneys. Its activity in blood rises in cholestatic and biliary conditions, and is also a sensitive (though not specific) marker of alcohol-related liver injury [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser on serum or plasma. It is collected in routine biochemistry as part of a liver panel, especially when investigating cholestasis, monitoring chronic liver disease, or following alcohol use. Results are reported in **U/L** (units of enzymatic activity per liter); LOINC's recommended UCUM code is **`[U]/L`** [2]. GGT activity does not have a JCTLM-listed reference measurement procedure with a SI molar concentration, because enzymes are reported as catalytic activity, not as substance concentration. The IFCC has published a reference procedure for GGT catalytic activity at 37 °C; in practice, all clinical labs report results in U/L (equivalently `IU/L`). ## Included Concepts The set contains a single LOINC "Lab Test" concept, measured in serum or plasma — by far the only specimen used in routine practice for this assay. - *3026910 / 2324-2 — Gamma glutamyl transferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)*: reported in U/L. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC: **`[U]/L`** (concept 8645). The notations `U/L` and `IU/L` are used interchangeably in clinical practice and represent the same value mathematically — no conversion is needed between them. ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name, but the following LOINC concepts that exist for GGT are deliberately not part of this set: - **DBS (Dried Blood Spot)** — `36032012 / 96593-9`. GGT measured on dried blood spot is not interchangeable with serum/plasma values (whole-blood matrix dilutes plasma enzymes by the haematocrit fraction, ≈ 40-45%). It is rarely used in clinical practice and could be added to a separate concept set in the future if needed. - **Other specimens**: amniotic fluid, body fluid, dialysis fluid, semen, urine, 24-hour urine — out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Macromolecular GGT presence** (`100741-8`) — a qualitative test for macro-GGT complexes, not a quantitative activity measurement. - **Ratios** like `Gamma glutamyl transferase/Aspartate aminotransferase` (`2325-9`) — out of scope. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "GGT", "gamma-GT", "γ-GT", "GGTP" or similar without further specification: `3026910 / 2324-2 — Gamma glutamyl transferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. This is by far the most widely used LOINC code for this test. - **Do not confuse with**: GGT/AST ratio, macromolecular GGT presence, or GGT in non-blood specimens (urine, body fluid, etc.) — all belong to other concept sets or are out of scope. - **Method is not part of the mapping target**: when the source records the assay method (kinetic colorimetric, IFCC reference at 37 °C, etc.), preserve it in the measurement source value — it does not change the target concept. - **Unit naming**: source systems may report `U/L` or `IU/L` (sometimes `iU/L`). All correspond to the same physical quantity. Map them all to the recommended unit `[U]/L` (concept 8645). ## References [1] Hall P, Cash J. What is the real function of the liver "function" tests? Ulster Med J. 2012;81(1):30-36. Available: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3609680/ [2] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 2324-2 — Gamma glutamyl transferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/2324-2/
